summary: End-to-end test of RE Template CRUD + Use Template sub-popup + UnitSelect. RE Template Create (3 items), Edit (modify desc + add 4th row), Delete all WORK. Use Template sub-popup inside Edit Rough Estimate fully functional: search filters template list, 'Use (Replace)' replaces scope items (toast 'Template X applied (1 items)'), 'Append' adds on top of existing (rows grew 1->2). UnitSelect dropdown opens correctly, option clicks select unit and reflect in button. One concern: UnitSelect search input does NOT accept keyboard text input under Playwright automation (value stays empty, 101 options still shown after type('kg'/'sqft'/'brick')) via page.keyboard.type, press_sequentially, .fill, or native setter + input event. Pre-existing hydration warning (<div> child of <tbody>) from DnD SortableContext still present (cosmetic).

ui_bugs.issue: Search input value does not update when text is typed programmatically (all methods tried: keyboard.type, press_sequentially, fill, native setter+input event). Options list never filters. Could be stopPropagation on onKeyDown/onClick interfering with React synthetic input events, OR the input is inside a React portal that the controlled state isn't receiving updates from in test env. User reports manual typing works, but this is fragile and inconsistent. Investigation suggested.

design_issues.screen: Edit Rough Estimate / Edit Template dialogs
issues: React hydration warning: '<div> cannot contain a nested tbody' - DnD SortableContext wrapper renders a <div> directly inside <tbody> in scope items table. Functional but noisy console.

action_items: (High) Verify UnitSelect search input truly accepts typed characters in browser - consider removing stopPropagation from onKeyDown on the input OR explicitly allowing typing keys; ensure onChange updates state. Automation could not reproduce successful typing across 4 different input strategies.
(Medium) Fix DnD SortableContext to not render a <div> directly inside <tbody> (use a custom strategy that renders as tbody/fragment).
critical_code_review_comments: RE Template CRUD works end-to-end (Create/Edit/Delete) with toasts, backend persistence, and correct totals.
Use Template sub-popup is well-implemented: search works for template picker, Replace vs Append both function correctly.
UnitSelect portal dropdown positioning and option click selection confirmed working again.

Concern: search input inside UnitSelect still shows automation-resistance. If the issue is also present for some users (e.g., keyboards with event interception), it would be a blocker. Please manually verify in a real browser with keyboard.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 test RE Template 'TEST_Standard 2BHK' (1200 sqft, 3->4 scope items). It was then edited and deleted during the test. 1 earlier duplicate 'TEST_Standard 2BHK' may remain in DB from the first partial run if auto-cleanup did not run - please check.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login via Demo Access > Super Admin. To reach RE Templates: /planning-board then click top-nav 'RE Templates'. To reach Edit Rough Estimate dialog: /crm/re-projects > click 'Approved' card > click edit icon (data-testid=edit-re-<id>) on a listed RE. Use Template button data-testid='use-template-btn'. Template picker dialog: data-testid='template-search-input', data-testid='template-option-<tid>', data-testid='apply-template-replace-<tid>', data-testid='apply-template-append-<tid>'. UnitSelect: trigger data-testid='unit-select', portal search 'unit-search-input', options 'unit-option-<value>'.

verified_fixes: Use Template sub-popup with Replace/Append works in RE Edit dialog
RE Template CRUD (Create/Edit/Delete) fully functional with backend persistence
UnitSelect portal dropdown opens, options are clickable, selection reflects in trigger button
Template auto-calculates estimated total from qty*rate
unresolved_observations: UnitSelect search input ignores programmatic text input - could be a real bug or automation limitation. Needs manual confirmation.
Hydration warning (div inside tbody) persists from DnD SortableContext - cosmetic.
